human_actions_pending.action: Run Main.tscn in Godot, verify 01-02 points 1-5, reply 'approved' or report issues
human_actions_pending.context: task-4 human-verify gate before plan completes

human_actions_pending.action: LIGHTING DECISION: confirm whether the ambient floor (ambient_light_energy=0.08) is sufficient for the MVP, OR request cross-space directional lighting (real day/night terminator while orbiting a planet, where the star is not in the render set). Not yet implemented.
human_actions_pending.context: In Planet space the star is not rendered (RND-02), so no point light is in frame; ambient floor is the MVP stand-in. Affects whether extra work is needed before/with 01-03.

decisions.decision: Render in scaled UNIT-space, not 1:1 meters: RenderBridge uses UniVec3.ToLocalDoubleUnits(ship) x per-space RenderFactor; camera far = 1e6 render units
decisions.rationale: 1:1-meters rendering forced far=1e12 which broke Godot's light culler (prepare_camera) and destroyed depth precision. Uniform unit-space scaling is visually equivalent but keeps far sane. SUPERSEDES locked decision D-15 ('honest 1:1 render distances').

decisions.decision: ToLocalDoubleUnits uses the OBSERVER's scale as the single per-frame unit basis (not the body's scale)
decisions.rationale: Bodies in one frame can have different scales; observer-basis keeps positions AND radii on one consistent basis (radius transform uses ship scale).

decisions.decision: User added UniVec3.ToDouble3Units() = (Double3)Units + Offset/Scale for precision (avoids Units*Scale meters round-trip); kept
Parent body rendered at -ship.LocalPos (frame origin); siblings via ToLocalDoubleUnits
decisions.rationale: parent.LocalPos is in the GRANDPARENT frame and cannot be diffed against ship.LocalPos (PARENT frame); the parent sits at the origin of the ship's frame.

decisions.decision: Per-space RenderFactor exports (Planet/Star/Galaxy/Universe), Planet/Star = 1e-8; Galaxy/Universe placeholders (not exercised by MVP scene)
decisions.rationale: User chose 'units + per-space factor' normalization so one camera far works at every tier.

decisions.decision: Removed DirectionalLight3D; skeleton used unshaded material; 01-02 added per-body lit planet materials, unshaded emissive star, shadowless OmniLight3D, glow, and ambient floor (0.08)
decisions.rationale: Light culler fix + per the plan's D-16/D-14; ambient floor keeps planets visible in Planet space where the star is not rendered.

decisions.decision: Skeleton viewing distance ShipOrbitMeters 7e6 -> 2.5e7; black Environment background
decisions.rationale: At 7e6 m the planet filled the whole FOV (white screen); 2.5e7 makes it read as a disc.

decisions.decision: Worktrees auto-degraded to sequential (no origin/HEAD); plans run sequentially on main tree
decisions.rationale: worktree base-check shouldDegrade=true in this local-only repo.

next_action: Ask the user to run Main.tscn and reply with (a) approval/issues on the 01-02 checkpoint and (b) the lighting decision (ambient floor vs cross-space directional terminator). On 'approved': run `gsd-tools query roadmap.update-plan-progress 01 01-02 complete`, commit tracking, then start Wave 3 (plan 01-03) by spawning a gsd-executor (model sonnet, sequential).
context_notes: Mid /gsd-execute-phase 1. Phase has 4 plans = 4 waves, fully sequential dependency chain, all autonomous:false (each ends in a human-verify checkpoint). Execution mode is SEQUENTIAL on the main tree (worktrees degraded). Wave 1 (01-01) took many iterations because the user redesigned the render architecture mid-plan (unit-space + per-space factor). When dispatching 01-03/01-04 executors, BRIEF them on the unit-space render architecture (it supersedes the original plan phrasing) and have them read 01-01-SUMMARY.md + 01-02-SUMMARY.md. Each executor: run auto tasks, stop at the human-verify checkpoint, write SUMMARY, do NOT mark complete or touch ROADMAP/STATE — the orchestrator finalizes after the user verifies.
